issue.title: Enhancement: Add 'Clear Scrollback' to terminal tab context menu + Ctrl+Shift+K shortcut (E1)

phases.title: Add terminalActionsRegistry, wire xterm.clear, context menu entry, and Ctrl+Shift+K
verification: stores/terminalStore.ts exports `terminalActionsRegistry` with register/unregister/clear methods, mirroring the existing terminalFocusRegistry shape.
Terminal.tsx imports `terminalActionsRegistry` from the same module as `terminalFocusRegistry`.
Terminal.tsx registers `clear: () => xterm.clear()` alongside the existing focus registration, and unregisters it on cleanup.
Terminal.tsx attachCustomKeyEventHandler has a new Ctrl+Shift+K branch that calls xterm.clear() and returns false, placed before the final `return true`.
TerminalTabs.tsx contextMenuItems array contains a `Clear Scrollback` entry between `rename` and the first separator.
The Clear Scrollback onClick uses `terminalActionsRegistry.clear(contextMenuTab.activeTerminalId)` — clearing only the active pane, not all panes in the tab.
If stores/index.ts is a barrel export for terminalStore, terminalActionsRegistry is exported the same way terminalFocusRegistry is (parity check).
Local TypeScript verification deferred to CI (`.github/workflows/pr.yml`).
Manual smoke: right-click a terminal tab — 'Clear Scrollback' appears between Rename and Split Right with the trash-can icon.
Manual smoke: click 'Clear Scrollback' — terminal display clears, scrollback gone, current prompt still visible.
Manual smoke: Ctrl+Shift+K from a focused terminal clears the active pane.
Manual smoke: TerminalSearch (Ctrl+F) right after a clear — no matches for previously-shown content.
Manual smoke: in a split-tabbed terminal, clear affects only the active pane (the other pane retains its scrollback).
